prompts.label: Simple one-page landing
prompts.text:
Create a simple one-page website for a beginner portfolio.

Requirements:
- One index.html and one styles.css (no frameworks yet)
- Semantic HTML: header, main, section, footer
- Hero with name, short tagline, and a CTA button
- About section, skills list, contact section
- Mobile-friendly layout (flexbox is fine)
- Clean, readable colors and spacing

Explain each file briefly and what I should open in the browser to preview.

prompts.label: Responsive navigation bar
prompts.text:
Build a responsive navigation bar for a small business site.

Requirements:
- Logo text on the left, links on the right (Home, About, Services, Contact)
- On screens under 768px, show a hamburger button that toggles the menu
- Use HTML + CSS only (vanilla JS allowed only for the menu toggle)
- Accessible: button has aria-label, focus styles on links

Provide complete code and short steps to test resizing in the browser.

prompts.label: Contact form (HTML)
prompts.text:
Create a contact form page for a beginner.

Fields: name (required), email (required, type email), subject, message (textarea, required).
- Use proper labels linked to inputs
- Basic HTML5 validation attributes
- A submit button (form action can be # for now)
- Simple CSS so the form looks neat and centered

Explain what still needs a backend later and what works without one.

prompts.label: Card layout with Flexbox
prompts.text:
Teach me Flexbox by building a row of 3 cards that wrap on small screens.

Each card: image placeholder, title, short description, "Read more" link.
- Use flexbox on the container (gap, wrap)
- Cards equal height on desktop
- Stack vertically on mobile

Give HTML + CSS with comments on the important flex properties.

prompts.label: Photo gallery with CSS Grid
prompts.text:
Create an image gallery page using CSS Grid (no frameworks).

- 6–9 placeholder images (use https://placehold.co or similar URLs)
- Responsive grid: 3 columns desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile
- Hover effect on images (slight scale or shadow)
- Page title and short intro paragraph

Include HTML + CSS and explain grid-template-columns and gap.

prompts.label: Light / dark theme toggle
prompts.text:
Add a light/dark theme toggle to a minimal sample page.

- Use CSS custom properties for colors (background, text, card)
- One button toggles theme
- Save choice in localStorage and apply on page load
- Keep code small and well-commented for a beginner

Provide index.html, styles.css, and script.js.

prompts.label: Reusable header and footer
prompts.text:
Show me how to structure a 3-page static site (home, about, contact) with the same header and footer on each page.

- Consistent navigation highlighting the current page
- Footer with copyright year and social links (placeholder #)
- Shared styles.css
- Explain whether I should duplicate HTML or use a simple include approach suitable for beginners

prompts.label: Mobile-first portfolio skeleton
prompts.text:
Create a mobile-first portfolio skeleton for a junior developer.

Sections: intro, skills (tags), 2–3 project cards with links, contact.
- Start CSS with mobile styles, then min-width media query for desktop
- No JavaScript required
- Neutral, professional look

List which files to create and what to customize with my own text.

prompts.label: FAQ accordion
prompts.text:
Build a FAQ section with 5 questions and accordion behavior.

- Click question to show/hide answer (only one open at a time is optional)
- Accessible markup (button or heading + panel)
- Simple CSS transitions if possible
- Vanilla JavaScript only

Provide full code and how to add my own questions.

prompts.label: Login page UI mockup
prompts.text:
Design a modern login page UI mockup (front-end only, no real auth).

- Email and password fields, "Remember me" checkbox, Submit button
- Link placeholders: Forgot password?, Sign up
- Centered card on a subtle background
- Responsive and keyboard-friendly focus states

Explain that this is UI only and what would be needed for real login later.

prompts.label: Skip-link accessibility pattern
prompts.text:
I'm improving accessibility on a plain HTML/CSS page.
Explain what a skip link is for keyboard users.
Provide a compact example: visually hidden anchor that appears on focus, linking to main#content.
Style it with minimal CSS (:focus-visible) and note one common pitfall beginners miss.
List how to test quickly with Tab in the browser.

prompts.label: Responsive pricing comparison table
prompts.text:
Build a semantic pricing table for three software plans using only HTML + CSS.
Requirements: thead with plan names, rows for limits/features, responsive horizontal scroll on small screens.
Add zebra striping without hurting readability.
Explain when a table beats a Flexbox/card layout for comparisons.
Tell me exactly which files you would change in a tiny static folder layout.

prompts.label: CSS-only hover tooltip
prompts.text:
Create an info icon beside a confusing label.
Requirements: hovering or focusing shows a small tooltip anchored near the trigger, using CSS only plus minimal HTML.
Discuss whether this is adequate for accessibility or why a richer pattern might help.
Explain z-index/stacking pitfalls for beginners.
Keep JavaScript optional for later.

prompts.label: Breadcrumb trail component
prompts.text:
Teach breadcrumbs for a fictional e-commerce hierarchy: Home › Books › Guides.
Use a nav landmark and an ordered/unordered pattern that screen readers tolerate well.
Style separators cleanly and show two responsive variants.
Contrast URL shape vs crumb labels.
Add a checklist for validating in devtools.

prompts.label: Sticky sidebar with flowing main column
prompts.text:
Lay out a docs-style page with a sidebar on desktop and stacked layout on narrow screens.
Use position: sticky so the sidebar summary stays visible while scrolling the article.
List parent overflow issues that commonly break sticky for beginners.
Provide HTML/CSS only and naming suggestions for landmarks.
Mention browser testing at two widths.

prompts.label: Print stylesheet for résumé
prompts.text:
I want a printable one-page-ish résumé that still renders nicely on screen.
Separate screen vs print CSS using @media print.
Goals: suppress navigation/background ornaments for print, sane margins and font sizing, prevent awkward splits across pages.
Walk through what to tweak in chrome print preview.
Give file organization advice for beginners.

prompts.label: prefers-color-scheme starter theme
prompts.text:
Prefer automatic light/dark styling using prefers-color-scheme without a toggle button.
Use CSS variables for core colors and typography.
Contrast this with a manual toggle driven by JS + localStorage (briefly).
Explain how to emulate both modes quickly in Chromium devtools.
Provide a miniature index + CSS example.

prompts.label: picture element responsive art direction
prompts.text:
Explain when to upgrade from a single responsive img srcset to picture with multiple sources.
Construct a concise example swapping aspect ratio/layout at md breakpoint.
Comment on decoding and loading attributes beginners should know.
List one mistake about order of source elements.
Keep copy friendly for someone who's only used css background images before.

prompts.label: prefers-reduced-motion safe animations
prompts.text:
I want tasteful motion (fade/slide) on my static site but I also care about vestibular sensitivity.
Teach prefers-reduced-motion and how to pair it with @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce).
Give a before/after CSS snippet for a hero entrance.
Explain how to test in browser devtools.
Point out one animation property that is usually safer than large translation jumps.

prompts.label: Long-form article semantics
prompts.text:
Sketch HTML for a blog post with title, byline, reading time placeholder, main article body, pull quote, and author aside.
Use article, header, time, blockquote, cite, figure/figcaption where appropriate.
Note one mistake people make confusing aside with sidebar ads only.
Keep CSS minimal but show typographic rhythm basics (max-width, line-height).
Finish with a short checklist for heading levels.
